site.title: Useful Tools
site.tagline: Free tools that run right in your browser. Your files never leave your device.

home.heading: Tools
home.subheading: Pick a tool and use it instantly.

qr.title: QR Code Generator
qr.description: Turn any link or text into a QR code and save it as PNG or SVG.
qr.emptyHint: Enter a URL or text above to see your QR code.
qr.ecHint: Higher levels survive more damage but make a denser code.
qr.lowContrast: Foreground/background contrast is too low — the code may not scan.
qr.invertedColors: Light foreground on a dark background may fail on some scanners.
qr.renderFailed: Could not generate the QR code. Please check your input.
qr.copyFailed: Copy isn’t supported in this browser. Use download instead.
qr.tooLong: Text is too long to fit in a QR code. Shorten it or lower the error-correction level.
qr.metaTitle: Free QR Code Generator — No Sign-Up, PNG & SVG
qr.metaDescription: Turn any URL or text into a QR code and download it as PNG or SVG. Free, no sign-up, generated right in your browser — nothing you type is sent to a server. Adjust error correction, size, and colors.
howTo: Enter the URL or text you want to turn into a QR code.
Adjust the error-correction level, size, margin, or colors if you like.
Download it as a PNG or SVG, or copy the image.
features: Free with no sign-up — the codes you create never expire.
Save as PNG or vector SVG (SVG is best for print).
Everything runs in your browser — nothing you type is sent to a server.

faq.a: No. You can create and download QR codes right away, with no sign-up or login.
No. These are static QR codes — the data lives inside the image itself, so they keep working forever.
Yes. Both PNG and SVG are free to download. For print, SVG is recommended because it scales to any size without losing quality.

faq.q: Is my data sent to a server?
faq.a: No. The QR code is generated in your browser, and the text or URL you enter is never sent to a server.

faq.q: Can I use it for print?
faq.a: Yes. Download an SVG so it stays sharp at any size. For printing, aim for at least 2×2 cm and 300 DPI.

wifi-qr-code.title: WiFi QR Code Generator
wifi-qr-code.description: Create a QR code that connects people to your WiFi network when scanned — no typing the password.
wifi-qr-code.metaTitle: Free WiFi QR Code Generator — Instant Network Connect
wifi-qr-code.metaDescription: Make a free WiFi QR code so guests join your network by scanning — no password typing. Enter your SSID and password; the code is generated in your browser and never sent to a server. Download PNG or SVG.
wifi-qr-code.emptyHint: Enter your network name to see the WiFi QR code.
wifi-qr-code.ecHint: Higher levels survive more damage but make a denser code.
wifi-qr-code.lowContrast: Foreground/background contrast is too low — the code may not scan.
wifi-qr-code.invertedColors: Light foreground on a dark background may fail on some scanners.
wifi-qr-code.renderFailed: Could not generate the QR code. Please check your input.
wifi-qr-code.tooLong: The details are too long to fit in a QR code. Shorten them or lower the error-correction level.
wifi-qr-code.copyFailed: Copy isn’t supported in this browser. Use download instead.
howTo: Enter your WiFi network name (SSID) and password.
Pick the encryption type — WPA works for almost all modern routers.
Download the code and print or display it. People scan it to connect.
features: People connect by scanning — no need to read out or type the password.
Free with no sign-up, and the code never expires.
Your network name and password stay in your browser and are never sent to a server.

faq.q: How does a WiFi QR code work?
faq.a: The code holds your network name, password, and encryption type. Most phone cameras read it and offer to join the network automatically.
Yes. The code is generated entirely in your browser — your SSID and password are never uploaded to a server.
Choose WPA for almost all modern routers (it covers WPA, WPA2, and WPA3). Use WEP only for old networks, or None for open networks.

faq.q: Does it work on iPhone and Android?
faq.a: Yes. iOS 11+ and modern Android read WiFi QR codes with the built-in camera app.
Yes. Download the SVG for a sharp print at any size — handy for a card on the table or a poster on the wall.

vcard-qr-code.title: vCard QR Code Generator
vcard-qr-code.description: Create a QR code that saves your contact details to a phone when scanned.
vcard-qr-code.metaTitle: Free vCard QR Code Generator — Contact Card QR
vcard-qr-code.metaDescription: Make a free vCard QR code that saves your name, phone, email, and more to a phone's contacts when scanned. Generated in your browser and never sent to a server. Download PNG or SVG.
vcard-qr-code.emptyHint: Enter a name or phone to see the contact QR code.
vcard-qr-code.ecHint: Higher levels survive more damage but make a denser code.
vcard-qr-code.lowContrast: Foreground/background contrast is too low — the code may not scan.
vcard-qr-code.invertedColors: Light foreground on a dark background may fail on some scanners.
vcard-qr-code.renderFailed: Could not generate the QR code. Please check your input.
vcard-qr-code.tooLong: The details are too long to fit in a QR code. Shorten them or lower the error-correction level.
vcard-qr-code.copyFailed: Copy isn’t supported in this browser. Use download instead.
howTo: Fill in the contact details you want to share.
Adjust the size or colors if you like.
Download the code. Scanning it adds the contact to a phone.
features: Scanning saves your details straight to a phone's contacts.
Free with no sign-up, and the code never expires.
Your contact details stay in your browser and are never sent to a server.

faq.q: What is a vCard QR code?
faq.a: It's a QR code containing contact details in the vCard format. Scanning it lets a phone save the contact in one tap.
Yes. The code is generated in your browser — nothing you enter is sent to a server.
At least a name and one way to reach you (phone or email). Organization, job title, and website are optional.

faq.q: Does it work on all phones?
faq.a: Yes. iPhone and Android read vCard QR codes with the built-in camera and offer to create a contact.

faq.q: Can I put it on a business card?
faq.a: Yes. Download the SVG so it stays sharp at print size, then add it to your card or email signature.
